albums.title: Evergreen In Your Mind
albums.sources: The Line of Best Fit · PopMatters
albums.description: Juni Habel presents the perilous beauty of nature as both metaphor and fact, to enlarge and reduce emotions and inhabit a liminal space wherein poetry resides. The album was reviewed positively by The Line of Best Fit on April 10, 2026.

albums.title: Cruel World
albums.sources: Exclaim! · The Line of Best Fit
albums.description: Humberstone comes into her own with 'Cruel World', creating an album that 'somewhat entrances' listeners. It received reviews on April 9, 2026, and April 13, 2026.

albums.title: Mellemtid
albums.description: Danish composer Emil Mark updates an older instrument, pipe organs, with modern technology in a minimalist approach on 'Mellemtid'. The album was reviewed on April 7, 2026.

albums.title: Fidelity
albums.description: Infused with Yaya Bey's sincerity, 'Fidelity' showcases her creative vision. The album was reviewed on April 13, 2026.

albums.title: Wor$t Girl in America
albums.description: Slayyyter's third full-length is described as a 'diamond-hard jawbreaker of a pop record, a totally self-immolating blaze of glory, a final roar before extinction'. This pop record was reviewed on April 7, 2026.

albums.title: Boycott Heaven
albums.description: The Format's first LP in 20 years, 'Boycott Heaven' finds them sidestepping the mainstream and landing where they belong. The review was published on April 8, 2026.

albums.title: ELEPHANT
albums.description: Adam O'Farrill's work on 'ELEPHANT' is described as 'simply brilliant,' showcasing his joyous and endlessly curious artistry. This jazz album received a review on April 8, 2026.

albums.title: Reunion Of Broken Parts
albums.description: This album is refreshingly contemporary, firmly rooted in the 2020s yet unafraid to draw inspiration from earlier eras, with clever arrangements and meticulous mixing. The review was published on April 13, 2026.
